title: Judgment Day
content:
Every day is Judgment Day,
    Count on no to-morrow.
    He who will not, when he may,
    Act to-day, to-day, to-day,
    Doth but borrow
    Sorrow.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham), titled "Judgment Day", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
